accepted_production_files_over_1200_line.reason: Pure outcome-blind control, benchmark, and missed-move selection freezes point-in-time eligibility before joining future outcomes.
accepted_production_files_over_1200_line.revisit_condition: Revisit when another control or benchmark family creates a cohesive boundary, with selection-digest parity tests in place.

accepted_production_files_over_1200_line.reason: Pure temporal baseline evaluator keeps cadence, return anchors, and feature evidence in one closed calculation path.
accepted_production_files_over_1200_line.revisit_condition: When adding another baseline family or changing the observation-spacing contract.

accepted_production_files_over_1200_line.reason: Pure partition-bounded episode and path outcome calculation keeps fixed-start grouping, horizons, expiry, and benchmark alignment under one frozen protocol.
accepted_production_files_over_1200_line.revisit_condition: Revisit when intraday outcomes, another horizon family, or a schema revision creates a cohesive new boundary.

accepted_production_files_over_1200_line.reason: Daily Operations is the single fail-closed transaction boundary for readiness, generation, doctor, publication, restart, rollback, and terminal receipts.
accepted_production_files_over_1200_line.revisit_condition: Revisit before another scheduler shares the transaction phases or cohesion becomes unclear.

accepted_production_files_over_1200_line.reason: Closed official-calendar acquisition keeps per-source authorization, immutable bytes, partial-coverage receipts, and validation in one fail-closed boundary.
accepted_production_files_over_1200_line.revisit_condition: Revisit when adding another source or status family and cohesion or review evidence supports a split.

accepted_production_files_over_1200_line.reason: Canonical campaign aggregation reconciles attempts, generations, outcomes, publication receipts, and current authority without provider activity.
accepted_production_files_over_1200_line.revisit_condition: When the campaign report schema changes or another campaign family needs the same aggregation primitives.

accepted_production_files_over_1200_line.reason: Central environment/config contract; splitting risks import-time default and env-var behavior drift.
accepted_production_files_over_1200_line.revisit_condition: When a dedicated config-v2 migration freeze and env snapshot tests exist.

accepted_production_files_over_1200_line.reason: Closed seven-file research-report projection centralizes exact run bindings, cross-report validation, anchored reads, bounded summaries, and byte-stable rendering.
accepted_production_files_over_1200_line.revisit_condition: Split when report schema v2 or another report family is introduced, preserving whole-bundle and byte-for-byte validation fixtures.

accepted_production_files_over_1200_line.reason: Pure descriptive episode analysis keeps fixed cohort, cost, monotonicity, operator-burden, and failure classification semantics together under one frozen protocol.
accepted_production_files_over_1200_line.revisit_condition: Split when the analysis schema changes or another cohort family is added, using digest-stable replay fixtures before moving helpers.

accepted_production_files_over_1200_line.reason: Read-once calendar snapshot validation keeps provenance, source coverage, secret checks, freshness, and publication projection in one security boundary.
accepted_production_files_over_1200_line.revisit_condition: When the live calendar container schema changes or a second publication format is introduced.

accepted_production_files_over_1200_line.reason: Pure frozen shadow-policy, recommendation-seal, final-test, and chronological walk-forward evaluation with no production-policy mutation path.
accepted_production_files_over_1200_line.revisit_condition: Revisit when another scenario family or recommendation-seal/walk-forward schema change creates a cohesive new boundary.

accepted_production_files_over_1200_line.reason: Route-gate decision logic is dense and behavior-critical for no-send notification eligibility.
accepted_production_files_over_1200_line.revisit_condition: When route-decision/gate snapshots cover every lane and quality-gate cap.

accepted_production_files_over_1200_line.reason: Static architecture report aggregator preserving compatibility aliases and existing gate counters.
accepted_production_files_over_1200_line.revisit_condition: Split when adding a new architecture report family or when report schema v2 removes historical aliases.

accepted_production_files_over_1200_line.reason: Historical CLI report compatibility binder with broad scanner-service monkeypatch expectations.
accepted_production_files_over_1200_line.revisit_condition: When config/report command bodies move to focused service modules.

accepted_production_files_over_1200_line.reason: The read-only health surface reconciles exact authority, maintenance, provider, request-ledger, and evidence-layer status without runtime inspection.
accepted_production_files_over_1200_line.revisit_condition: When health sections have independent golden render fixtures or the system-page contract reaches v2.

accepted_production_files_over_1200_line.reason: Safety-critical no-send generation orchestrator owns one bounded provider call and fail-closed publication assembly.
accepted_production_files_over_1200_line.revisit_condition: When adding another live-safe market provider or changing the generation transaction boundary.

accepted_production_files_over_1200_line.reason: Pure bounded targeted-review selection keeps outcome-aware categories, evidence bindings, deterministic ranking, and queue finalization in one policy-neutral path.
accepted_production_files_over_1200_line.revisit_condition: Split when another review category family or feedback-ledger integration is proposed, with frozen queue-digest fixtures first.

accepted_production_files_over_1200_line.reason: Stable argparse flag bundle; splitting individual flag groups risks CLI default drift.
accepted_production_files_over_1200_line.revisit_condition: Next parser feature addition or when event-alpha flag groups can be snapshot-tested per submodule.

accepted_production_files_over_1200_line.reason: Deterministic derivatives crowding evaluator with tightly coupled fixture smoke coverage.
accepted_production_files_over_1200_line.revisit_condition: When adding a new derivatives metric family or crowding class.

accepted_production_files_over_1200_line.reason: Provider/cache enrichment flow is a cohesive behavior-critical boundary.
accepted_production_files_over_1200_line.revisit_condition: When adding a new enrichment source or cache policy.

accepted_production_files_over_1200_line.reason: The read-only calendar page keeps coverage, receipt, temporal, filter, and event-card truth in one server-rendered surface.
accepted_production_files_over_1200_line.revisit_condition: When the calendar page gains a new interaction family and has byte-stable page-section fixtures.

accepted_production_files_over_1200_line.reason: Static deleted-shim/tombstone registry and report writer; large by design and non-behavioral.
accepted_production_files_over_1200_line.revisit_condition: When deleted-shim reporting can be split from old-import linting without changing gate output.

accepted_production_files_over_1200_line.reason: Legacy notification-plan compatibility core; no-send semantics are more important than churn.
accepted_production_files_over_1200_line.revisit_condition: When notification plan rows are covered by schema-level golden fixtures.

accepted_production_files_over_1200_line.reason: Canonical operator-state hashing and exact revision ownership remain centralized and fail closed.
accepted_production_files_over_1200_line.revisit_condition: When operator-state schema v2 can split digest policy from revision persistence with byte-stable fixtures.

accepted_production_files_over_1200_line.reason: Central escaped dashboard route renderer preserves shared candidate filtering, exact authority badges, and fail-closed response layout across the read-only surface.
accepted_production_files_over_1200_line.revisit_condition: Split the remaining candidate and authority helpers when a dashboard interaction family changes, using page-level golden render fixtures.

api_artifact_doctor_core_note: Behavior-compatible doctor implementation preserved while public artifact_doctor.py is the small orchestrator.

accepted_class_exceptions.exception_reason: SQLite migration ownership is intentionally centralized to avoid untested schema drift.
accepted_class_exceptions.owner_note: DB schema behavior must not change in this cleanup pass.
accepted_class_exceptions.revisit_condition: Revisit only with explicit migration tests and backup/restore verification.

accepted_class_exceptions.exception_reason: Signal persistence methods share schema assumptions, row serialization, and outcome lookup behavior.
accepted_class_exceptions.owner_note: Avoid splitting storage write paths without SQLite roundtrip parity tests.
accepted_class_exceptions.revisit_condition: Revisit when storage schema v2 or a repository layer is introduced.

accepted_class_exceptions.exception_reason: Watchlist persistence methods are stable DB helpers and only slightly exceed the advisory limit.
accepted_class_exceptions.owner_note: No DB schema or paper/watchlist behavior changes in this architecture-maintenance pass.
accepted_class_exceptions.revisit_condition: Revisit when watchlist storage grows new tables or migrations.

provider_class_split_status.owner_note: Provider activation safety and cohesion take precedence over advisory line counts.
provider_class_split_status.reason: Fixture/live market enrichment adapter is tightly coupled to request budgeting and no-live defaults.
provider_class_split_status.revisit_condition: Revisit when watchlist market enrichment gains another provider implementation.

provider_class_split_status.owner_note: Keep current timeout/429 behavior stable; public-provider noise is expected.
provider_class_split_status.reason: No-key public provider is fail-soft and only slightly over the advisory limit.
provider_class_split_status.revisit_condition: Revisit when adding a second GDELT mode or durable request ledger.

provider_class_split_status.owner_note: Split only when another prediction-market provider is added.
provider_class_split_status.reason: Small no-key prediction-market provider keeps fixture/live parsing in one stable adapter.
provider_class_split_status.revisit_condition: Revisit when Polymarket Gamma support grows beyond the current parser.

provider_class_split_status.owner_note: Keep feed failure semantics stable unless adding a reusable RSS client layer.
provider_class_split_status.reason: RSS/Atom adapter is tightly coupled to per-feed fail-soft behavior and source normalization.
provider_class_split_status.revisit_condition: Revisit when project-blog sources get persistent request ledgers or richer feed classes.

storage_mixin_exception_status.owner_note: DB schema behavior must not change in this cleanup pass.
storage_mixin_exception_status.reason: SQLite migration ownership is intentionally centralized to avoid untested schema drift.
storage_mixin_exception_status.revisit_condition: Revisit only with explicit migration tests and backup/restore verification.

storage_mixin_exception_status.owner_note: Avoid splitting storage write paths without SQLite roundtrip parity tests.
storage_mixin_exception_status.reason: Signal persistence methods share schema assumptions, row serialization, and outcome lookup behavior.
storage_mixin_exception_status.revisit_condition: Revisit when storage schema v2 or a repository layer is introduced.

storage_mixin_exception_status.owner_note: No DB schema or paper/watchlist behavior changes in this architecture-maintenance pass.
storage_mixin_exception_status.reason: Watchlist persistence methods are stable DB helpers and only slightly exceed the advisory limit.
storage_mixin_exception_status.revisit_condition: Revisit when watchlist storage grows new tables or migrations.

dead_duplicate_code_removal_note: Non-public top-level Event Alpha shims listed in research/EVENT_ALPHA_DELETED_SHIMS.json were removed after shim dependency and old-import reports proved they were unused internally.

deprecation_plan.policy: No flat Event Alpha public compatibility shims remain; scanner.py remains the historical CLI entrypoint and new work imports canonical package paths.
Deleted old Event Alpha imports are allowed to fail; docs show canonical paths and tombstone tests cover deleted paths.
Any future public compatibility bridge may warn in development mode only after an accepted compatibility-breaking migration and release notes.
Any future public old import bridge can be removed only through an explicit compatibility-breaking migration with full verification and release notes.

line_gates.risk: Burning down the transitional compatibility core can change CLI defaults, Make target behavior, provider guardrails, or research-only side-effect gates if moved without command snapshots.

line_gates.next_migration_module: tests/rsi, tests/cli, and tests/event_alpha for any remaining umbrella-only cases
line_gates.risk: Over-aggressive removal could break the standalone compatibility runner expected by Make and AGENTS.md.

line_gates.next_migration_module: crypto_rsi_scanner/event_alpha/doctor/checks/safety.py, namespace.py, stale_artifacts.py, and focused historical-row counter plugins
line_gates.risk: Doctor extraction can silently change strict/WARN semantics, report counter names, or stale namespace handling if compatibility tests do not pin output.
